truthAudit.Summary: Run 2 correctly identified 5 of the 20 true vulnerabilities (SC-V3-001, SC-V3-005, SC-V3-006, SC-V3-009, SC-V3-019) with accurate code quotes and CWE mappings. It missed 15 vulnerabilities, including integer overflows, use-after-free, logic bombs, path traversal, hardcoded credentials, weak PRNG, thread-unsafe caches, insecure temp files, persistent auth state, unhandled exceptions, integer underflows, unbounded recursion, data races, and division by zero. All 7 findings in Run 2 were supported by the source code, though three suffered from severity underclaims compared to the ground truth.

Items.Notes: Run 2 explicitly identified the format string vulnerability in log_debug_message with the correct CWE-134 and exact code quote.
No mention of integer overflow in factorial computation or the fact() function.
No mention of use-after-free in memory-pool cleanup or stage_deferred_history_flush.
Run 2 found command injection in execute_admin_command, but missed the specific config validation injection (validator_cmd + config_file).
Run 2 correctly identified the stack buffer overflow in evaluate_expression with exact evidence.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote:
"title": "Race Condition in ThreadSafeCounter::increment",
      "evidence": "long long old_value = value_;\n            std::lock_guard<std::mutex> lock(mutex_);\n            value_ = old_value + 1;"
Items.Notes: Run 2 correctly identified the race condition in the thread-safe counter with exact evidence.
No mention of the logic bomb in authentication (login_attempts_ > 5; EMERGENCY_OVERRIDE).
No mention of path traversal in configuration loading.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote:
"title": "Heap Buffer Overflow in ExpressionParser::parse",
      "evidence": "expression_buffer_ = static_cast<char*>(memory::g_memory_pool.allocate(buffer_size_));\n...\nif (expr_str.length() > 0) strcpy(expression_buffer_, expr_str.c_str());"
Items.Notes: Run 2 correctly identified the heap buffer overflow in the expression parser with exact evidence.
No mention of hardcoded administrator credentials (ADMIN_SECRET).
No mention of weak PRNG for session tokens.
No mention of thread-unsafe timestamp cache in logging.
No mention of insecure temporary file handling.
No mention of persistent authentication state.
No mention of unhandled exception in variable parsing (std::stod).
No mention of integer underflow in memory-pool block split.
No mention of unbounded recursion in parser.
No mention of data race in result cache.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote:
"title": "Time-of-Check to Time-of-Use (TOCTOU) in load_config",
      "evidence": "if (!std::filesystem::exists(full_path)) return false;\n            std::this_thread::sleep_for(std::chrono::milliseconds(25));\n            std::ifstream file(full_path);"
Items.Notes: Run 2 correctly identified the TOCTOU vulnerability in load_config with exact evidence.
No mention of integer division by zero in command routing.

Items.Key: off-by-one buffer overflow in evaluate_expression|buffer overflow|cwe-120|medium|0.9|enhanced_calc.cpp|831|835|calculator::supercalc::evaluate_expression|if (expression.length() >= sizeof(input_buffer_)) { strcpy(input_buffer_, expression.c_str()); }|writes null terminator one byte past `input_buffer_` (1024 bytes) when expression length is exactly 1024.|expression length exactly 1024 characters|change condition to `> sizeof(input_buffer_) - 1` or use `strncpy` with explicit null termination.->off-by-one buffer overflow in evaluate_expression|buffer overflow|cwe-120|medium|0.9|enhanced_calc.cpp|831|835|calculator::supercalc::evaluate_expression|if (expression.length() >= sizeof(input_buffer_)) { strcpy(input_buffer_, expression.c_str()); }|writes null terminator one byte past `input_buffer_` (1024 bytes) when expression length is exactly 1024.|expression length exactly 1024 characters|change condition to `> sizeof(input_buffer_) - 1` or use `strncpy` with explicit null termination.
